steps.title: Match the age-pattern claim
steps.content: The claim says participation peaked in a certain age range from 1992 to 1995 and then declined among older participants. Supporting data should compare a younger group with an older group across those years, showing that the older group had fewer participants after the peak-age group.

steps.title: Reject non-age comparisons
steps.content: A misstates the adult-to-Brownie comparison. B tracks Brownies over time but does not compare younger and older participants. C makes a Daisies-to-Adults comparison the table does not support across the period. The answer is D.
